briefdescription.para: Construct a zero size (0 × 0).
Construct a size from explicit width and height.

detaileddescription.para:
Negative inputs are clamped to 0.
width
The desired width (clamped to >= 0).
height
The desired height (clamped to >= 0).

briefdescription.para: Construct a size from the axis-aligned distance between two positions.

simplesect.para: The order of the positions does not matter; absolute differences are used.

briefdescription.para: Equality comparison on width and height.
Inequality comparison on width and height.
Add two sizes using saturated arithmetic.

simplesect.para: The component-wise sum, capped at the maximum coordinate value.

briefdescription.para: Subtract two sizes using saturated arithmetic.

simplesect.para: The component-wise difference, clamped to non-negative values.

briefdescription.para: Add a size using saturated arithmetic.
Subtract a size using saturated arithmetic.
Get the width (>= 0).
Set the width.

detaileddescription.para:
Negative values are clamped to 0.
width
New width (clamped to >= 0).

briefdescription.para: Get the height (>= 0).
Set the height.

detaileddescription.para:
Negative values are clamped to 0.
height
New height (clamped to >= 0).

briefdescription.para: Get the size component for the selected orientation.

parameterdescription.para: The orientation that selects width or height.

briefdescription.para: Compute a position inside the rectangle defined by this size for a given anchor.

detaileddescription.para:
Bottom-right resolves to (width-1, height-1), top-left to (0,0), etc.
anchor
The anchor describing the target corner/edge/center.
The position inside the [0,width-1]×[0,height-1] grid (or (0,0) for empty dimensions).

briefdescription.para: Compute the offset for content aligned inside this size.

detaileddescription.para:
If contentSize is larger than this size on an axis, the returned offset on that axis is negative.
contentSize
The aligned content size.
alignment
The alignment for the content.
The zero-based offset for placing the content inside this size.

briefdescription.para: Test if this size is zero.
Check if this size fits completely into another size (component-wise <=).

simplesect.para: true if width <= other.width and height <= other.height.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size minimum, const Size maximum) const noexcept
memberdef.name: isInRange

briefdescription.para: Test if the width and height of this size is in the given range.

simplesect.para: true If this size is in the range minimum-maximum.

briefdescription.para: Check if a position lies strictly inside the bounds [0,width) × [0,height).

simplesect.para: true if 0 <= x < width and 0 <= y < height.

briefdescription.para: Add another size using saturated arithmetic.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size other, const Orientation orientation) noexcept
memberdef.name: add

briefdescription.para: Add another size only on the selected axis using saturated arithmetic.
Subtract another size using saturated arithmetic and clamp the result to zero.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size other, const Orientation orientation) noexcept
memberdef.name: subtract

briefdescription.para: Subtract another size only on the selected axis using saturated arithmetic and clamp the result to zero.
Expand this size so it is at least the given size.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size other, const Orientation orientation) noexcept
memberdef.name: expandTo

briefdescription.para: Expand this size so it is at least the given size on the selected axis.
Limit this size so it is at most the given size.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size other, const Orientation orientation) noexcept
memberdef.name: limitTo

briefdescription.para: Limit this size so it is at most the given size on the selected axis.
Create a copy expanded so it is at least the given size.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size other, const Orientation orientation) const noexcept
memberdef.name: expandedWith

briefdescription.para: Create a copy expanded so it is at least the given size on the selected axis.
Create a copy limited so it is at most the given size.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size other, const Orientation orientation) const noexcept
memberdef.name: limitedWith

briefdescription.para: Create a copy limited so it is at most the given size on the selected axis.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size minimum, const Size maximum) const noexcept
memberdef.name: clampTo

briefdescription.para: Component-wise clamp with a minimum and maximum size.

simplesect.para: If minimum.width > maximum.width or minimum.height > maximum.height, the behavior is undefined.
a size (min.width <= width <= max.width, min.height <= height <= max.height)

briefdescription.para: Clamp a position inside this size.

detaileddescription.para: The resulting position is at least (0, 0) and less than (width, height).

briefdescription.para: Compute the area (width * height).

simplesect.para: The area. Note: returns 0 if either dimension is 0.

briefdescription.para: Convert a 2D position to a row-major linear index.

parameterdescription.para: The position. Behavior is undefined if not contained by this size.

briefdescription.para: Visit all positions inside the size in row-major order.

parameterdescription.para: The function to invoke for each Position (x from 0..width-1, y from 0..height-1).

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size minimum, const Size maximum) const noexcept
memberdef.name: componentClamp

briefdescription.para: Get the maximum size that can be represented by this type.
Get the minimum size that can be represented by this type.
Get a size.
Compute the absolute difference between two integer values.
The maximum valid x coordinate inside the size, or 0 if width == 0.
Half of the valid x range endpoint used for horizontal center positioning.
The maximum valid y coordinate inside the size, or 0 if height == 0.
Half of the valid y range endpoint used for vertical center positioning.
A non-negative 2D size (width × height).

listitem.para: Width and height are clamped to be >= 0.
Many operations assume a grid indexed from (0,0) to (width-1,height-1).
